tweets.text: What do you wish you had known earlier? As we come to the end of #WomensHistoryMonth, @UnderSecStateJ and @UnderSecT reflect on their roles as women in leadership and share how they overcame professional hurdles facing women in the workplace. https://t.co/cRs4bdN2hb

During this #WomensHistoryMonth we recognize Eugenie Anderson, America’s first woman ambassador. She never feared standing up to communist authorities and believed in people-to-people diplomacy to build strong relationships between nations. More here: https://t.co/w4VBYiOmUL. https://t.co/F59A4HcVyb

.@StateDeptSpox: On Transgender Day of Visibility, the United States affirms that transgender rights are human rights. Today and every day, we reaffirm our commitment to promoting and protecting the freedom, dignity, and equality of all persons. https://t.co/0r0eb4HYbB

.@StateDeptSpox: We are warning U.S. citizens that Russian government security officials in both Russia and in Ukraine may be singling out U.S. citizens based on their nationality. We reiterate that all U.S. citizens in Russia and Ukraine should depart immediately. https://t.co/KYpOAYBxbc

.@StateDeptSpox: I want to honor the courage and fortitude of independent journalists in Russia. These men and women continue to risk their livelihoods and their freedom to ensure their compatriots still have access to rigorous and professional journalism — to the truth. https://t.co/FfLeWjo6S6
Live now! @StateDeptSpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/3sSt1oIJzi. https://t.co/kGRs4QDQcc

.@SecBlinken: Farmers in Ukraine, instead of tending to their crops, have been forced to either fight for their country’s future and independence, or to flee. Ships with grain are not getting out from Black Sea ports because they’re blocked by Russia’s aggression. https://t.co/FARw0otip3

Today on Transgender Day of Visibility we are announcing a historic milestone: U.S. citizens will be able to select an X as their gender marker on their U.S. passport application starting April 11. Read more here: https://t.co/rputpu0cNU. https://t.co/I8cOG0zXkv
We continue our work to ensure that all transgender, nonbinary, and gender non-conforming persons are able to live safely and feel included. Trans rights = human rights. #TDOV https://t.co/uBSrxlbJJ9

This #WomensHistoryMonth, @UnderSecStateJ remembers her sister Rena Golden, who was a pioneering journalist in the field of cable news. “She opened the front door to many others, so being a ‘first’ was no longer a big deal and it became much more the norm.” https://t.co/FtdBf0fmzi

In the latest Science Speaks DipNote, @StateGWI’s Dr. Aubrey Paris explores One Health—a collaborative approach to public health that recognizes the intersections between the health of humans, animals, & the environment—& how it impacts women & our world. https://t.co/A7OYXgPk6h

Today, @USAsiaPacific Assistant Secretary Kritenbrink and @State_SCA Assistant Secretary Lu joined their Quad Senior Official counterparts from 🇦🇺 🇮🇳 🇯🇵 to discuss our shared vision of a free and open Indo-Pacific. https://t.co/NMuyQbkcjg

The United States has growing cultural and economic relations with Algeria, which we see at both the government-to-government and people-to-people levels, and more and more U.S. companies are seeking investment in Algeria. https://t.co/vn0ztLozQq

The United States and Algeria share a long history – from the 1795 Treaty of Peace and Amity to hosting negotiations to release American hostages from Tehran in 1981 – and our bilateral relationship continues to flourish. https://t.co/i6kNmaxel5 https://t.co/Md4qmMVwx8

For the second year in a row, the State Department is teaming up with @NASEFedu for #Farmcraft2022 to foster learning about farming challenges and tools. Register here. https://t.co/x3T8GyDXHf | Learn more. https://t.co/GGXR8AmyEZ

.@POTUS’s message to the Russian people: This war is not worthy of you. Putin can and must end this war. The American people stand with you and the brave citizens of Ukraine who want peace. https://t.co/PsurzRGJtt

When an earthquake struck Haiti on August 14, 2021, the U.S. government quickly stepped in to deliver aid and save lives. Learn more about the United States' immediate and ongoing efforts to support our Haitian partners in the latest DipNote blog from @StateDeptPM.

At the White House, @POTUS and Prime Minister @leehsienloong discussed the U.S.-Singapore bilateral relationship, including strengthening cooperation on health security, defense and security, space cooperation, and people-to-people ties. #USSG55

.@DeputySecState: Russia’s bombardment of Ukraine’s cities and critical infrastructure has created one of the fastest-growing humanitarian crises. Russian forces have laid siege to cities like Mariupol, where citizens have been left without food, water, heat, or electricity. https://t.co/fzdx8CerpR

Live now! @DeputySecState Wendy Sherman delivers remarks on the humanitarian impact of Russia’s war against Ukraine, including on global food security, at the @UN Security Council, also streaming on https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz and https://t.co/Mdkkxqa9uz. https://t.co/FJu8EdYoDl

.@POTUS: International sanctions are sapping Russian strength, its ability to replenish its military, and its ability to project power. And it is Vladimir Putin who is to blame. https://t.co/TPEdCCpEPB

#DYK? Morocco has received 2.45 million doses of Pfizer vaccine from the U.S. contribution to #COVAX, a global initiative aimed at ensuring equitable vaccine distribution, in the last several months, as well as more than 300,000 doses of the J&J vaccine earlier in 2021. https://t.co/tKq1KgRjZJ

Applications for the 9th annual #P3Impact Award are now open. The award, hosted by @GPatState, @ConcordiaSummit, and @UVA, recognizes and honor best practices of public-private partnerships that are improving communities in the most impactful ways. https://t.co/miFbVZNX9h

Over the past year, the Morocco-Israel diplomatic relationship has blossomed with the historic re-opening of the Israeli liaison office in Rabat. The U.S. welcomes’s Morocco’s role in promoting regional stability, prosperity & the historic normalization of relations with Israel. https://t.co/1lpYIBU8m0

The strategic bilateral partnership between the United States and Morocco is rooted in shared interests in regional peace, security, and prosperity. Learn more about the U.S.-Morocco bilateral relationship: https://t.co/Zb4UIorMee https://t.co/OL0p627Evv

Lucile Atcherson is our #WomensHistoryMonth honorary of the day, who broke barriers and paved the way for future women as a diplomatic officer. Read more on her diplomacy work: https://t.co/kLgDIsTqBS. https://t.co/Pf3E4lMZCa
.@POTUS' message to the people of Ukraine: We stand with you. Full remarks: https://t.co/dBwy2S3ujv. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/Mj98NBmxjq
In Poland, @POTUS took a moment to honor the life and memory of former Secretary of State @Madeleine Albright, who passed away last week at the age of 84. https://t.co/JDDlpHUiVL

The Biden-Harris Administration's FY 2023 Budget will:
✅Support Ukraine and our Allies and partners
✅Affirm our Alliances and renew our global leadership 
✅Address the climate crisis
✅Advance our leadership in global health
✅Defend democracy globally 
https://t.co/AQbn1SIyNA
A month since Putin’s war of choice and in the face of continued aggression, the United States, alongside our Allies and partners, remains #UnitedWithUkraine. https://t.co/3lm2YKwMRf

There is simply no justification or provocation for Russia’s choice of war. It’s an example of one of the oldest of human impulses: using brute force and disinformation to satisfy a craving for absolute power and control. https://t.co/BXaLRjUp6k

We have an update from Stanislav in Ukraine, who details the resilience he has seen from his fellow Ukrainians in the face of Kremlin aggression: “We are more willing to die than to give up or lose. And that is why the Russian Federation has already lost in this war.” https://t.co/GsxX6rbm3z

To keep our world safe, we must work together to secure nuclear material and prevent nuclear materials from falling into the wrong hands. More on the first ever #ACPPNM Conference at the @iaeaorg in Vienna: https://t.co/f5RX3DHp8W

.@POTUS' message to the people of Russia: This is not who you are. This is not the future you deserve for your families and your children. I’m telling you the truth: This war is not worthy of you, the Russian people. Putin can and must end this war. https://t.co/a4EYb9gBfo

Since April 2021, the U.S. has provided over half a billion dollars in aid to the Palestinian people, including $417 million+ in humanitarian assistance through @UNRWA, $75 million in aid @USAID, and $20.5 million in COVID and Gaza recovery assistance. https://t.co/9w0i29dNTl
Americans and Israelis are united by our shared commitment to democracy, economic prosperity, and regional security. Our partnership has never been stronger. https://t.co/jR0IKRjy23

As we continue to highlight #WomensHistoryMonth, @UnderSecT for Arms Control & International Security Bonnie Jenkins shares more about the women who inspire her life. “In life or in careers, it’s very important to believe in oneself.” https://t.co/Ww0mbM7N6a

.@POTUS: Rather than breaking Ukrainian resolve, Russia’s brutal tactics have strengthened the resolve. Rather than driving @NATO apart, the West is now stronger and more united than it has ever been. https://t.co/gu5HQY2EB6
Subscribe to The Week at State, a weekly email newsletter of all the news you missed from the Department of State. https://t.co/gNUp8OngPT https://t.co/r0PyqhPjzl

The United States is unwavering in our commitment to Israel’s security, and regional stability. This includes addressing Iran’s destabilizing behavior in the region, and never allowing Iran to acquire a nuclear weapon. https://t.co/9EJPRWHAaF

Today’s #WomensHistoryMonth honoree is Barbara Watson. She was the first African American and the first woman to serve as an assistant secretary. She paved the way for black diplomats and leaders. We honor her service with the Department. https://t.co/NUXReknjXd

In the month since the Kremlin’s aggression against Ukraine began, the United States has delivered $1.36 billion in military assistance, almost $293 million in humanitarian assistance, and sanctioned over 470 aggressors and 196 entities across 9 sectors. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/wMvXhONKth
“We need the world’s help now. We are fighting for our freedom and everybody’s watching.” #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/Pe7BNZVLT9

.@POTUS on defending @NATO territory: “In addition to the 100,000 U.S. forces now stationed in Europe to defend NATO territory, NATO established, as you already know, four new battle groups in Romania, Hungary, Bulgaria, and Slovakia to reinforce the eastern front.” https://t.co/AHSpeOszj8

This week, the United States partnered with @gavi #COVAX ​and the African Vaccine Acquisition Trust to donate more than 2.4 million COVID-19 vaccine doses to four countries in Sub-Saharan Africa. https://t.co/FxbBe1cKdL

.@StateDeputySpox addresses the unprecedented refugee crisis that has resulted from the Kremlin’s unjustified and unprovoked invasion of Ukraine, and also condemns the DPRK’s recent ICBM test. Read more in the full briefing transcript: https://t.co/9sEnZGWnsQ

In the latest DipNote blog from @StateDeptPM, learn how the Department of State and @DeptOfDefense are working together to support vulnerable populations — including Afghan women and girls — as part of the United States' Afghan evacuation and resettlement efforts.

While in Brussels, Belgium for the @NATO Summit, @POTUS outlined the security and humanitarian assistance the United States is providing to the Ukrainian government and people. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/wde5InBqJG

Since Putin started the war in Ukraine, the Kremlin’s unprovoked aggression has killed thousands of its own soldiers and countless Ukrainians. We remain #UnitedWithUkraine in our ongoing assistance and call on the Russian government to end this war. https://t.co/DiboJZ5y9V

In the latest from our Ukrainian Voices series, we hear from Oleksandr, who lost his hands to battle in 2014, shares how he continues to work to defend Ukraine from Russian aggression. “The war came to every part of Ukraine.” #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/ahEkag43GD

This is in addition to the $800 million recent package: https://t.co/lkWuptEMRj. The U.S. is committed to providing security assistance to Ukraine to help them defend their country against the Kremlin’s unprovoked and unjustified invasion. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/nqq7RRyR4u

The United States will provide more than $1 billion in new funding towards humanitarian assistance for those affected by Putin’s war in Ukraine. The U.S. is the largest single donor of humanitarian, democracy, and human rights assistance to Ukraine. More: https://t.co/Ga5WyGTFkG
To learn more about the #ACPPNM and how it reinforces global nuclear security, check out the latest DipNote blog from @StateISN:

Secretary @Madeleine Albright's leadership and influence on American diplomacy was remarkable. Read more on her amazing legacy and the iconic more than 200 pins she collected: https://t.co/ecffM45Gmr https://t.co/rM90i9KELP

.@POTUS speaking in Brussels, Belgium: “@NATO has never been more united than it is today. Putin is getting exactly the opposite of what he intended to have as a consequence of going into Ukraine.” Full remarks: https://t.co/TO1M997Wxe. https://t.co/5NgdW5xjcu

In the month since the Kremlin’s aggression against Ukraine began, the United States has delivered $1.36 billion in military assistance, almost $293 million in humanitarian assistance, and sanctioned over 470 aggressors and 196 entities across 9 sectors. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/9SDq0KWPDQ

The United States is working to ensure the Russian government and the Lukashenka regime in Belarus pay a severe economic and diplomatic price for their unprovoked aggression against Ukraine. Here’s a look at our comprehensive actions to date: https://t.co/zQeOULjby8.

.@State_E and @SciDiplomacyUSA announced U.S. government support for the Eastern Tropical Pacific Marine Corridor, one of the most biodiverse areas in the ocean, at the High-Level Dialogue with Colombia, Costa Rica, Ecuador, and Panama this week. https://t.co/DSzNrZFDqb

We mourn the passing of @Madeleine Albright and extend condolences to her family and colleagues. She served as the 64th U.S. Secretary of State, the first woman to hold this position, and mentored a generation of diplomats. Her legacy will live on. https://t.co/itlZJKVmR3 https://t.co/1NzjRIgLav

Ambassador at Large for Global Criminal Justice Beth Van Schaack provides background on the assessment that Russia forces are committing war crimes in Ukraine. “We are committed to pursuing full accountability for war crimes in Ukraine using all of the tools available to us.” https://t.co/KA0mcv2lBx
Live now! @StateDeptSpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/3VY2Qigxsd. https://t.co/wwS3OL0qPY
.@POTUS: The United States and our Allies will defend every inch of territory of @NATO countries with the full force of our collective power. https://t.co/u9hFatn9xV

Long before its unprovoked war in Ukraine, the Kremlin created websites that masquerade as independent news sites—but really spread disinformation. @ShareAmerica sheds light on this disinformation tactic meant to undermine democracy: https://t.co/1bMEV8iOw6

On this #WorldWaterDay, @SciDiplomacyUSA Assistant Secretary Medina explains how water is essential for conserving nature. Read her latest DipNote blog highlighting the importance of water and U.S. efforts to strengthen global water security. https://t.co/JAmeOSbtQb https://t.co/fV7LPmMKvw
“We need the world’s help now. We are fighting for our freedom and everybody’s watching.” #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/TPWsu6z0b4

.@StateDeptSpox: The United States strongly condemns the abhorrent terrorist attack today in Be'er Sheva, Israel. We offer our deepest condolences to the victims and their families and our prayers for the full recovery of those wounded. https://t.co/DrhZBpzgpE

.@StateDeptSpox on Ukraine: Satellite images show whole areas of Mariupol in ruins, with smoke surrounding the city. It is time to stop the fighting now, to give the opportunity for peace to take root. It is time to end this absurd conflict of Putin’s choosing. https://t.co/y1OaLedEvN

.@WhiteHouse National Security Advisor Jake Sullivan provides an overview of @POTUS’s trip this week to consult with @NATO Allies: “The West has been united, the President is traveling to Europe to ensure we stay united, to cement our collective resolve.” #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/DsN0YfcY0X
Live now! @StateDeptSpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/d5YtRs9Vuv. https://t.co/QMSKO6FT2A

Water security is essential to addressing the climate crisis. Through the Global Water Strategy, the State Department collaborates with other U.S. government agencies to build a water-secure world. #WorldWaterDay https://t.co/CehNbPR1xl https://t.co/teeeFxyp1I

.@SecBlinken discusses U.S. support to help meet the immediate humanitarian needs of Rohingya. We are working alongside allies and partners to promote accountability for those responsible for these atrocities. https://t.co/cATwSPqpJZ

.@SecBlinken: The U.S. reaffirms its broader commitment to helping Rohingya on this path out of genocide—toward truth, toward accountability & toward a home that will welcome them as equal members, and respect their human rights and dignity, alongside that of all people in Burma. https://t.co/C1T4yTACy1

.@StateDeptSpox: We support the people of Ukraine in their just cause—the defense of their country and their democracy. We’ll continue to support Ukraine’s efforts to de-escalate through diplomacy in order to secure a ceasefire and the withdrawal of Russia’s troops from Ukraine. https://t.co/STupg61qxW

.@StateDeptSpox on the determination that the Burmese military committed genocide: We reiterate our call for the Burmese military regime to end its use of violence, release all those unjustly detained, and engage in constructive dialogue to restore Burma’s path to democracy. https://t.co/ShHBG8zUwe
Live now! @StateDeptSpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/nz3WoSBixN. https://t.co/12a754gldJ

.@State_E Jose Fernandez & @EnergyAtState’s Harry Kamian will travel to Paris, March 23-25, to represent the U.S. at the #IEAMinisterial. The focus will be on addressing energy security concerns, in the wake of the Kremlin’s unprovoked invasion of Ukraine. https://t.co/6wrr9vvZjY

.@SecBlinken shares findings that determined what was being done to the Rohingya was a genocide on innocent people in Burma. “We must remember that behind each of these numbers are countless individual acts of cruelty and inhumanity.” https://t.co/NEz7V7htX2

.@SecBlinken on Burma: Beyond the Holocaust, the United States has concluded that genocide was committed seven times. Today marks the eighth, as I have determined that members of the Burmese military committed genocide and crimes against humanity against Rohingya. https://t.co/Eht93zFE8K
Live now! @SecBlinken delivers remarks at the United States @HolocaustMuseum in Washington, D.C., following his tour of the exhibit “Burma’s Path to Genocide.” https://t.co/H3WfbGLnu8

Today at approximately 9:30 a.m. EDT, @SecBlinken will deliver remarks at the United States @HolocaustMuseum in Washington, D.C., following his tour of the exhibit “Burma’s Path to Genocide.” Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz, and https://t.co/CNynaB7vTC.
Many humanitarian organizations are providing urgent assistance to those affected by the crisis in Ukraine. Here are some ways you can help from wherever you are: https://t.co/1NlBjb5o9N

This week, the United States—in partnership with @gavi #COVAX and the African Vaccine Acquisition Trust—donated more than 2.7 million safe and effective COVID-19 vaccine doses to four countries in Sub-Saharan Africa. https://t.co/g3XQ0N3Gbu
In cities across the world, global citizens are speaking out against Russian aggression and showing unity and support for the people of Ukraine. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/fbvq2TXYgV
.@SecBlinken's message to Russian citizens: You do not deserve a pointless war with your neighbors, friends, and family in Ukraine. https://t.co/FPhjQCbcBK

Happy #Nowruz! Today we extend greetings to all those celebrating this joyous occasion to mark the arrival of Spring and a new year. May this time of reflection bring brighter days. https://t.co/WoUQOtYeHx

.@SecBlinken’s message to Ukraine’s government and people: “The courage that they are showing every day, the leadership that they are showing to the world, it’s inspiring. And the world is here; the world is with you.” https://t.co/JvE2jA5yeu

This week, the United States—in partnership with @gavi #COVAX and the African Vaccine Acquisition Trust—donated more than 2.7 million safe and effective COVID-19 vaccine doses to four countries in Sub-Saharan Africa. https://t.co/Wzvmqc9wGP

This week, in partnership with #COVAX, the U.S. donated another 349,830 safe and effective Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to the people of Honduras. With 500 million doses donated globally thus far, we are making a major impact on the COVID-19 pandemic in Honduras and worldwide. https://t.co/xwl4gVQCjP

During today’s briefing, @StateDeputySpox reiterated that there are no U.S. soldiers in Ukraine, expressed condolences for the private U.S. citizen killed in Ukraine, and called for an immediate end to Russia's senseless war. Read the full transcript here: https://t.co/8WZeph1FPe

.@POTUS: Democracies are rising to meet this moment, rallying the world to the side of peace and the side of security. We’re showing our strength, and we will not falter. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/azNjOS8sdT

This #WomensHistoryMonth we honor the first woman to serve as U.S. Secretary of State, @madeleine. Her influence on American diplomacy is remarkable. Check out @NMADmuseum's online exhibit that features more than 200 pins that Madeleine collected. https://t.co/ecffM45Gmr https://t.co/W6MQkBpD3L

Wishing everyone who celebrates a happy and colorful Holi. Holi is one of the most celebrated festivals in South Asia and is also called the festival of colors. To all celebrating and attending festivals, have a safe and fun time filled with love and peace. #HappyHoli https://t.co/bZ12bJiVoE

Phạm Đoan Trang of Vietnam is an internationally recognized author, blogger, journalist, and human rights defender. She is being honored as a #WomenofCourage for bravely reporting on social issues previously untouched by the Vietnamese media. https://t.co/IpbsfC6p62

Roegchanda Pascoe is being recognized as a #WomenOfCourage for her advocacy for peace, justice and economic inclusion in South Africa. She continues to create safer communities, for women and children who have been traumatized by violence, & train the next generation of leaders. https://t.co/7lodW7pPZN

Carmen Gheroghe is the president of E-Romnja, an NGO that promotes Roma women rights. She is being honored as a #WomenOfCourage for her tireless work with numerous organizations in Romania and efforts to promote the rights and empowerment of Roma women and girls. https://t.co/zhMSOzAAxk

.@SecBlinken: We will continue to stand with Ukraine as it fights for its future. We will continue to increase the costs on Russia until it ends this war of choice. And we will continue to provide life-saving aid to the Ukrainian people. https://t.co/xfsh01Br8u
.@SecBlinken: "The support we’re providing Ukraine, as well as our financial and economic measures against Moscow, will strengthen Ukraine’s hand at the diplomatic table." https://t.co/EtYKEDXcaX
“President Putin’s invasion of Ukraine is not going to plan.” @SecBlinken discusses what the Russian Government could do next. https://t.co/H4tCZxavvv
.@SecBlinken explains how the Kremlin is manufacturing narratives about chemical weapons, then falsely blaming Ukraine to justify potential escalations of attacks. https://t.co/lZ47M0UiAF

.@SecBlinken discusses the ongoing war on in Ukraine and injuries and lives loss due to Russia’s constant attacks on innocent civilians, including children. “Intentionally targeting civilians is a war crime.” https://t.co/4skNVWJ6PB

When Americans team up with people in other parts of the world tackling similar challenges, everyone gains new tools, new insights, and new perspectives. To learn more and get involved in citizen diplomacy with @StateIVLP, visit https://t.co/i1io6DP9Qw.
Live now! @SecBlinken delivers remarks to the press. https://t.co/qZv2POqmKd
Today at approximately 1:30 p.m. EDT, @SecBlinken will deliver remarks to the press. Watch live here on Twitter, https://t.co/58Uc9lIgDz, and https://t.co/EFYV59gGKq.

500 million. That’s how many safe and effective COVID-19 vaccine doses the United States has now shared with the world. If we set these vials end-to-end, they would stretch almost 3,500 km from Doha to Kathmandu.

And we are just getting started. https://t.co/v3BZo86iLG

The United States has now donated 500 million safe and effective COVID-19 vaccine doses to over 110 countries, more than any other nation in the world. We continue to share these doses at no cost, with the sole purpose of saving lives now. More here: https://t.co/QYQ3Y3hz40. https://t.co/1wTcJ73NEo
Each year on March 17, we celebrate the people of Ireland and the many ways Irish influence has shaped our own nation. Happy #StPatricksDay! https://t.co/SascsELX2I

Ukraine is home to centuries-old, culturally unique historical and architectural landmarks. The U.S. stands with Ukraine, including efforts to protect cultural heritage in the face of the Kremlin’s horrific attacks: https://t.co/nDssJi28Gp
The United States does not have chemical or biological weapons programs in Ukraine. The Russian government needs to #StopTheLies. https://t.co/IA1a4dL5k9
.@SecBlinken on Russia and Ukraine: "The picture is very clear; it couldn't be more clear. We have an aggressor; we have a victim." #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/KiMvNiJxGw

As @SecBlinken explained on @NPR, the situation in Ukraine affects us all, because the basic principles that Russia is violating, that Russia is aggressing, are the principles necessary to keep international peace, security, and stability. #UnitedWithUkraine

.@State_E will travel to Honduras and Costa Rica to promote U.S. investment and meet with Honduran President @XiomaraCastroZ and the Presidents of Alliance for Democracy in Development, @CarlosAlvQ, @LuisAbinader, and @NitoCortizo. https://t.co/nIqFbq9oTf

Bhumika Shrestha of Nepal is being honored as a #WomenOfCourage for advocating for the advancement of gender minorities’ rights and social justice. Her advocacy work with the Government of Nepal has helped ensure the needs of the LGBTQI+ community are met at the policy level. https://t.co/u0MRRoTe9k

Doina Gherman served in the Moldovan Parliament since 2019 as a #WomenOfCourage, advocating for women’s participation and inclusion. She has championed efforts to promote women’s inclusion and protect survivors of domestic and gender-based violence. https://t.co/2Cr9SRPh51

Najla Mangoush of Libya is being honored as a #WomenOfCourage for her work as the first woman Foreign Minister of Libya. She is a lawyer by training and a conflict-resolution expert with a long history of working for peace. https://t.co/dfVYwr1n2v

.@StateDeptSpox: As the President said today, America stands with the forces of freedom in Ukraine. We are leading the effort to isolate President Putin on the global stage, while simultaneously strengthening Ukraine’s hand at the negotiating table. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/7g0P7nqVNC

.@POTUS announces an additional $800 million in assistance for Ukraine: "The world is United in our support for Ukraine and in our determination to make Putin pay a heavy price. America is leading this effort, together with our Allies and partners." #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/QCGglWH6An
Live now! @StateDeptSpox briefs reporters from the State Department, also streaming on https://t.co/Ln79eX77in. https://t.co/TT7C5BHsfT
Live now! @POTUS delivers remarks on the assistance the United States is providing to Ukraine, also streaming at https://t.co/IZPEo0b53M.   https://t.co/NmtzR23L0W
The United States remains committed to Ukraine's independence, sovereignty, and territorial integrity. Read @SecBlinken's interview with @wolfblitzer on @CNN: https://t.co/t1fWzNYNJR
"Stop the war. Stop the killing. Stop the destruction." More from @SecBlinken's message to President Putin and his @CNN interview: https://t.co/t1fWzNYNJR https://t.co/l84jq1EUCm

Working together with other nations to save lives is a priority for the United States. In partnership with #COVAX, we donated nearly 2 million Pfizer COVID-19 vaccine doses to Uzbekistan. To date, we have provided to Uzbekistan a total of more than 7.6 million doses. https://t.co/nre3rcojI3
.@POTUS: Putin is an aggressor and Putin must pay the price. He cannot pursue a war that threatens the very foundations of international peace and stability. https://t.co/Swhofn9ArR

In the latest DipNote blog, @SciDiplomacyUSA Assistant Secretary Medina outlines the global crisis of plastic pollution and explains the historic progress made to combat it at the @UN Environment Assembly in Nairobi, Kenya. Read more: https://t.co/ePMMNNMBZW

.@StateDeptSpox on the 11-year anniversary of the Syrian uprising: As we stand with the Syrian people, we continue our efforts to secure a nationwide ceasefire, expand access to humanitarian aid, achieve justice and accountability, and reach a political resolution. https://t.co/mbfwOWa91i

.@SecBlinken: Tomorrow, I'll travel to Brussels, Poland, and the Baltics. .@SecBlinken: The United States is continuing our diplomatic efforts. We are keeping the door open to a diplomatic solution. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/alBKzMlf1w

.@SecBlinken:  Today, we are also imposing sweeping sanctions on Russia’s defense sector.  All told, these sanctions and restrictions have had a powerful effect on Russia’s economy. The value of President Putin’s “war fund” has vanished. https://t.co/T7wRbs2LQ7

.@SecBlinken:  We are holding Russia accountable and isolating Russia’s economy. We’ve sanctioned most of Russia’s largest financial institutions and its sovereign wealth fund. We’ve restricted Russia’s ability to seek funding beyond its borders. https://t.co/LpDMrvGhNW
.@SecBlinken: We’re sending humanitarian assistance to the people of Ukraine, and we are working to support the frontline countries. #UnitedWithUkraine https://t.co/CKOvZ6VbHW
